column1: # Tab-separated manifest of every rename committed in the 2026-05-20
#   - "(deleted)" as NEW means OLD has no replacement (delete-only rename)
# Used by scripts/check-rename.sh --plan to verify every rename has
# been fully swept across all 14 layers (Go, tests, yaml, env, SQL, UI,
# i18n, prod EnvironmentFile, prod DB rows, docs, skills, rules, test
# Maintainer rule: when adding a rename to the migration, append a line
# here in the SAME PR. Reviewers must reject the PR if the rename does
# not appear in this manifest AND/OR `scripts/check-rename.sh --plan`
# reports leftover references after the PR's sweep.
# CAVEATS — script limitations the operator must handle manually:
#   1. Word-boundary fixed-string matching: short names like `PORT` will
#      The operator must review each L1-L14 hit by hand for these.
#      where the path spans nested keys) cannot be detected by a single-
#      line grep. Manually verify the yaml restructure for these.
#   3. UI i18n keys may legitimately mention old configKey names inside
#      translation values (e.g. "Cache Config" might be a label). Review
#      L8 hits to distinguish "key references the old name" (must fix)
#      vs "translation value happens to contain the word" (acceptable).
# leaf field renames inside nested yaml — script reports best-effort matches;
# manually verify the surrounding nested structure was also moved
# delete-only env renames (replaced by Redis universal schema in PR-3)
